task_completion.description: Create Putaway Rule for Rotor Blade in Blade Rack 1 - WP
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Putaway Rule form or list view showing Item 'Rotor Blade', Warehouse 'Blade Rack 1 - WP', Capacity '40', and Priority '1'.

task_completion.description: Create Putaway Rule for Rotor Blade in Blade Rack 2 - WP
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Putaway Rule form or list view showing Item 'Rotor Blade', Warehouse 'Blade Rack 2 - WP', Capacity '40', and Priority '2'.

task_completion.description: Create Putaway Rule for Gearbox in Heavy Parts - WP
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Putaway Rule form or list view showing Item 'Gearbox', Warehouse 'Heavy Parts - WP', Capacity '50', and Priority '1'.

task_completion.description: Create a Purchase Receipt from the Eagle Hardware Purchase Order
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Purchase Receipt form being created with Supplier 'Eagle Hardware', populated with items 'Rotor Blade' and 'Gearbox'.
Look for the 'Apply Putaway Rule' checkbox being ticked in the Purchase Receipt form before submission.

task_completion.description: Save and Submit the Purchase Receipt with correctly split items
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the final Purchase Receipt showing a 'Submitted' status badge. The items table must show the system automatically split the items: one row for 40 Rotor Blades in 'Blade Rack 1 - WP', one row for 20 Rotor Blades in 'Blade Rack 2 - WP', and one row for 25 Gearboxes in 'Heavy Parts - WP'.

integrity.description: Agent used the ERPNext web GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent navigated the ERPNext interface in a web browser to create rules and receipts, rather than using terminal commands, python scripts, or direct API calls.
Look for the 'Submitted' status badge on the Purchase Receipt. It should not remain in 'Draft' status.

integrity.description: Agent used the automated putaway feature instead of manual entry
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent checked 'Apply Putaway Rule' to let the system split the rows, rather than manually calculating the capacities and adding separate rows for the split quantities themselves.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Supplier: 'Eagle Hardware'. Items: 'Rotor Blade', 'Gearbox'. Warehouses: 'Blade Rack 1 - WP', 'Blade Rack 2 - WP', 'Heavy Parts - WP'. The final submitted Purchase Receipt should show 60 Rotor Blades automatically split into 40 in 'Blade Rack 1 - WP' and 20 in 'Blade Rack 2 - WP', and 25 Gearboxes in 'Heavy Parts - WP'.
